claims.label: Lemma 1
claims.statement: For any k-strong equilibrium sigma*, every coalition S of size at most k cannot improve all members simultaneously.
claims.conclusion: No coalition of size <= k can jointly deviate to improve all members
claims.proof_technique: By contradiction: assume a profitable deviation exists, derive contradiction with equilibrium condition.
claims.proof: Suppose coalition S deviates to sigma_S. By k-SE definition, some player i in S has u_i(sigma*) >= u_i(sigma_S, sigma*_{-S}). Contradiction.

claims.label: Theorem 1
claims.statement: The Price of Anarchy of any k-strong equilibrium is bounded by (1 - 1/e)^{-1} when k = O(log n).
claims.proof_technique: Smoothness argument combined with coalition-resistance bound.
claims.proof: Apply smoothness framework to k-SE. Use Lemma 1 to bound deviation gain. Compute ratio against optimal.

claims.label: Corollary 1
claims.statement: For k = 1, the PoA bound of Theorem 1 recovers the standard Price of Anarchy bound for Nash Equilibria.
claims.conclusion: Theorem 1 generalises the classical PoA bound when k = 1
claims.proof_technique: Direct substitution of k = 1 into Theorem 1.
claims.proof: Set k = 1 in Theorem 1. k-SE reduces to standard NE. Bound matches classical result.

attributes.condition: Theorem 1 proof invokes Lemma 1 to bound coalition deviation
Corollary is obtained by substituting k=1 into Theorem 1
